title: Roast carrots
subtitle: Halved, oiled, roasted until tender and caramelised
excerpt: Carrots halved lengthways, tossed in oil with thyme, and roasted at high heat until the cut surface caramelises and the carrots are tender throughout. A simple side that takes 30 minutes and fits in the oven alongside the main roast. Honey is optional but improves the glaze.
sourceNotes: Roasting rather than boiling carrots became standard in British home cooking in the 1990s as the Sunday roast expanded to fill more of the oven. Roasted carrots have a more concentrated flavour and a better texture than boiled ones for most purposes.

content.text: Halving the carrots lengthways exposes more surface area to the heat, which produces caramelisation on the cut face. Whole carrots roast fine but take longer and do not caramelise as well. Larger carrots can be cut into quarters.

Heat the oven to 200°C. Toss the {{carrot}} with {{olive-oil}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, {{black-pepper}}, and {{thyme}} if using. Spread in a single layer, cut-side down, in a roasting tin. Roast for 25 minutes. If using {{honey}}, drizzle it over the carrots, turn them, and return to the oven for a further 5 minutes until glazed. The cut surface should be golden and caramelised.

items.symptom: Soft and steamed, no colour on the cut surface
items.cause: Overcrowded tin, or cut surface not in contact with the tin
items.fix: Place cut-side down for the first 25 minutes. Ensure there is space between each piece — crowded carrots steam each other

items.symptom: Dried out and leathery on the outside, still hard in the centre
items.cause: Oven too hot, or carrots too thick
items.fix: For thick carrots, reduce to 180°C and give them 35 minutes. The exterior should caramelise gently, not char

content.text: Roast carrots are a standard Sunday roast side across British cooking. They can share the oven with the main roast, going in around the same time as the potatoes and coming out when done. The honey-glazed version is the standard restaurant presentation; the plain version is the standard home version.
